title: AI ROI: Automate Customer Value Not Internal Productivity - LinkedIn
search_path_label: A 级媒体 / GDELT 路径
full_text:
I have been saying that 𝘁𝗼𝗸𝗲𝗻𝗺𝗮𝘅𝘅𝗶𝗻𝗴 is a self-serving trend manufactured by the frontier labs that will soon meet the reality of enterprise leaders needing to show fast ROI.
I have been saying that 𝘁𝗼𝗸𝗲𝗻𝗺𝗮𝘅𝘅𝗶𝗻𝗴 is a self-serving trend manufactured by the frontier labs that will soon meet the reality of enterprise leaders needing to show fast ROI. Great to see Axios reporting today how that has started:
𝗨𝘀𝗲 𝗰𝗮𝘀𝗲𝘀: "Most people default to automating tasks they dislike rather than tasks most valuable to the company. Instead, they should focus on using AI to drive revenue." Revenue comes from expanding at existing customers and acquiring new customers. This means let's automate the workflows that wow customers ahead of internal productivity.
𝗖𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀: "Employees were using AI models to check the weather. That gets expensive fast: Enterprise AI plans are not truly all-you-can-eat, and even simple chatbot queries can carry heavy token costs." Measure outcomes and not just tokens so that you understand the ROI equation fast.
𝗛𝘂𝗺𝗮𝗻𝘀: "We are the bottleneck to more efficient adoption, as we're still catching up on AI. Leadership isn't always helping: Throwing AI licenses at the wall and seeing what sticks aka thousand-flowers-bloom approach isn't leading to tangible returns." AI coding agents continuously being prompted by developers on their local machine is not the same as always-on, long-running, background agents that correlate across 100s of live data sources. When such agents achieve outcomes without tokenmaxxing, we remove the human bottleneck.
𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮: "When enterprises are hesitant to give AI agents unfettered access to proprietary data, those agents become less effective" I would argue the right answer here is 𝘇𝗲𝗿𝗼-𝘁𝗿𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗮𝗰𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀 as opposed to unfettered access. That goes to the root of why enterprises are hesitant.
Answer to all this angst is 𝗽𝘂𝗿𝗽𝗼𝘀𝗲-𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝘁 𝗔𝗜 where non-deterministic agents with their domain-specific prompts, skills, tools, subagents, memory, sandboxes, governance policies are powering deterministic high ROI workflows. We at Autoheal focus on these 3 such workflows:
1. Root cause analysis of production incidents to reduce 𝗠𝗧𝗧𝗥
2. Rapid alert triage to identify incident signals early to reduce 𝗠𝗧𝗧𝗗
3. Closing the loop with proactive prevention to reduce 𝗠𝗲𝗮𝗻 𝗧𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝗧𝗼 𝗣𝗿𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 (𝗠𝗧𝗧𝗣)
https://lnkd.in/gXCTtAHg
AI sticker shock hits corporate America

# AI ROI: Automate Customer Value Not Internal Productivity - LinkedIn

- raw_id: R-070
- original_url: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/sidchoudhury_ai-sticker-shock-hits-corporate-america-activity-7465815556051345408-rrvV
- source_name: keyword search / Anysearch
- collected_at: 2026-07-01T02:33:05.463Z

## clean_text

I have been saying that 𝘁𝗼𝗸𝗲𝗻𝗺𝗮𝘅𝘅𝗶𝗻𝗴 is a self-serving trend manufactured by the frontier labs that will soon meet the reality of enterprise leaders needing to show fast ROI.
I have been saying that 𝘁𝗼𝗸𝗲𝗻𝗺𝗮𝘅𝘅𝗶𝗻𝗴 is a self-serving trend manufactured by the frontier labs that will soon meet the reality of enterprise leaders needing to show fast ROI. Great to see Axios reporting today how that has started:
𝗨𝘀𝗲 𝗰𝗮𝘀𝗲𝘀: "Most people default to automating tasks they dislike rather than tasks most valuable to the company. Instead, they should focus on using AI to drive revenue." Revenue comes from expanding at existing customers and acquiring new customers. This means let's automate the workflows that wow customers ahead of internal productivity.
𝗖𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀: "Employees were using AI models to check the weather. That gets expensive fast: Enterprise AI plans are not truly all-you-can-eat, and even simple chatbot queries can carry heavy token costs." Measure outcomes and not just tokens so that you understand the ROI equation fast.
𝗛𝘂𝗺𝗮𝗻𝘀: "We are the bottleneck to more efficient adoption, as we're still catching up on AI. Leadership isn't always helping: Throwing AI licenses at the wall and seeing what sticks aka thousand-flowers-bloom approach isn't leading to tangible returns." AI coding agents continuously being prompted by developers on their local machine is not the same as always-on, long-running, background agents that correlate across 100s of live data sources. When such agents achieve outcomes without tokenmaxxing, we remove the human bottleneck.
𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮: "When enterprises are hesitant to give AI agents unfettered access to proprietary data, those agents become less effective" I would argue the right answer here is 𝘇𝗲𝗿𝗼-𝘁𝗿𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗮𝗰𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀 as opposed to unfettered access. That goes to the root of why enterprises are hesitant.
Answer to all this angst is 𝗽𝘂𝗿𝗽𝗼𝘀𝗲-𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝘁 𝗔𝗜 where non-deterministic agents with their domain-specific prompts, skills, tools, subagents, memory, sandboxes, governance policies are powering deterministic high ROI workflows. We at Autoheal focus on these 3 such workflows:
1. Root cause analysis of production incidents to reduce 𝗠𝗧𝗧𝗥
2. Rapid alert triage to identify incident signals early to reduce 𝗠𝗧𝗧𝗗
3. Closing the loop with proactive prevention to reduce 𝗠𝗲𝗮𝗻 𝗧𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝗧𝗼 𝗣𝗿𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 (𝗠𝗧𝗧𝗣)

𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮: "When enterprises are hesitant to give AI agents unfettered access to proprietary data, those agents become less effective" I would argue the right answer here is 𝘇𝗲𝗿𝗼-𝘁𝗿𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗮𝗰𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀 as opposed to unfettered access. That goes to the root of why enterprises are hesitant.
Answer to all this angst is 𝗽𝘂𝗿𝗽𝗼𝘀𝗲-𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝘁 𝗔𝗜 where non-deterministic agents with their domain-specific prompts, skills, tools, subagents, memory, sandboxes, governance policies are powering deterministic high ROI workflows. We at Autoheal focus on these 3 such workflows:
1. Root cause analysis of production incidents to reduce 𝗠𝗧𝗧𝗥
2. Rapid alert triage to identify incident signals early to reduce 𝗠𝗧𝗧𝗗
3. Closing the loop with proactive prevention to reduce 𝗠𝗲𝗮𝗻 𝗧𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝗧𝗼 𝗣𝗿𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 (𝗠𝗧𝗧𝗣)
